population.name: Congenital LQT2 (KCNH2/hERG loss of function) — illustrative

extraction.method: illustrative congenital long-QT background: the dominant channel defect applied as a mean conductance shift under the shared illustrative CVs

notes: HYPOTHESIS-TIER — DO NOT USE FOR PREDICTION. KCNH2 (hERG) loss of function reduces the rapid delayed-rectifier IKr (mean shift IKrx0.5), here recentering the illustrative population spread on a reduced-repolarization-reserve background. The magnitude is an illustrative heterozygous-scale shift, NOT a genotype-calibrated parameter; qNet/APD thresholds remain the HEALTHY reference. A mechanism demonstration of the gene-drug repolarization-reserve interaction, never a per-patient or per-genotype safety claim. Every assessment is capped at Tier D and labelled non-predictive.
